stories.title: What has the U.S. war with Iran accomplished?
stories.description: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

stories.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
stories.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

stories.title: U.S., Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race
stories.description: The U.S. reached a last-minute ceasefire with Iran just before Trump's deadline for the country to meet his demands. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins the U.S. House race in Georgia.

stories.title: How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy
stories.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Tomas {TAHM-us} Philipson, a former senior economic adviser in President Trump's first term, about the impact of the president's wartime threats on the global economy.

stories.title: A fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire shows cracks as attacks continue across the region
stories.description: Iran and the U.S. and Israel said they would suspend strikes but countries in the region continued to report attacks and Israel said it would not stop its assault in Lebanon.

stories.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
stories.description: A US 15-point plan and an Iranian 10-point variant are oceans apart, writes the BBC's diplomatic correspondent.

stories.title: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.description: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.

stories.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
stories.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

stories.title: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty
stories.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
stories.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

stories.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
stories.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

stories.title: Trump condemned over threat that Iran's 'civilisation will die'
stories.description: The UN's chief says he is "deeply troubled" by such statements, as the US president ramps up pressure on Iran to agree a deal to end the war.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: Pakistan plays major role trying to mediate an end to Iran war
stories.description: Pakistan has emerged as a key mediator between the US and Iran, playing a crucial role in brokering the two-week ceasefire agreement and working with both camps, as well as regional powers, to end the war. Islamabad has ties to Tehran and Washington and is set to hold talks this ...

stories.title: Netanyahu fails to address Israel’s failure to achieve war goals in Iran
stories.description: Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky says that when Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu finally made a statement on the US-Iran ceasefire, he failed to “address the fact that Israel has not achieved any of its stated war goals, which included regime change in Iran...

stories.title: US-Iran ceasefire: Why Tehran wants to charge ships for crossing the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: As part of its 10-point proposal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran, Tehran is pushing for ongoing control over the vital Strait of Hormuz – including charging what officials suggest could be as much as $2 million per ship to help the country rebuild its bombed-out infrastructure ...

stories.title: Middle East live: France blasts Israel’s ‘unacceptable’ Lebanon strikes, ceasefire hangs in balance
stories.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Thursday condemned Israel’s "unacceptable" strikes on Lebanon, which killed 182 people and wounded 890 the day after the US and Iran reached a two-week ceasefire agreement. As Iran and Hezbollah retaliated for the attacks, Barrot warned...

stories.title: Iran-US truce on brink as Israel pounds Lebanon, Tehran warns of escalation
stories.description: A fragile two-week truce between Iran and the United States hung in the balance on Wednesday as Tehran threatened renewed hostilities and Israel launched heavy strikes on Lebanon. Both sides claimed victory, but at least 182 people were killed and nearly 900 wounded, Lebanese aut...

stories.title: Trump meets NATO’s Rutte as US weighs exit over Iran war
stories.description: US President Donald Trump held talks with NATO chief Mark Rutte on Wednesday, the White House said, as he weighed leaving the alliance after it did not join the US in its war against Iran. The Wall Street Journal reported that Trump was also considering withdrawing US troops from...

stories.title: At least 182 killed as Israel strikes central Beirut after saying Iran truce doesn't apply there
stories.description: Lebanon’s health ministry said that Israeli strikes on Wednesday killed 182 people, the highest single-day death toll in the Israel-Hezbollah war. Israel launched a barrage of strikes in central Beirut and elsewhere in the country as a shaky ceasefire took effect between the U.S....

stories.title: 'Pakistani military calling the shots: Has connections with IRCG, very good relationship with Trump'
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Ali Vaez, Director of Iran Project & Senior Advisor at the International Crisis Group. While the US sees the outcome as a decisive military victory, Vaez argues that the more consequential story lies in the reconfiguration of power within Iran. ...

stories.title: Energy crisis not over despite relief rally
stories.description: Oil prices have plunged and stock markets soared after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Israel and Iran. But regardless of whether the deal holds, the energy crisis unleashed by the war is far from over. Damage to infrastructure in the region means it c...

stories.title: US-Iran truce: ‘Sensitive behind-the-scenes conversations’ paved way for Pakistan-brokered ceasefire
stories.description: François Picard welcomes Mumtaz Zahra Baloch, Pakistan’s ambassador to France and Permanent Delegate to UNESCO. According to Baloch, the truce between Iran and the US is less about outright victory and more about survival – and a recalibration of power. She notes that what we are...

stories.title: Can the ceasefire hold? Fragile US–Iran truce tested ahead of talks
stories.description: No apocalypse. Instead, an eleventh-hour ceasefire between Iran and the United States – one that is still being tested – with both sides claiming victory after five and a half weeks of war that may reshape how the world views them.

stories.title: Trump administration's claims of victory in Iran 'really quite hollow', analyst says
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Jennie Shin, Barbara Slavin, Distinguished Fellow at the Stimson Center, says that "the Trump administration is desperately seeking a way to end the war and declare victory. The US can stop its attacks for a while but its claims of victory are really qui...

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel ready to 'return to battle at any moment' against Iran
stories.description: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel remains prepared to confront Iran if necessary, despite a truce reached between the United States and Iran. Lebanon's civil defence service said 254 ​people had been killed in Israel's strikes across Lebanon on...

stories.title: ‘Unhinged, insane’: US lawmakers call for Trump’s removal via 25th Amendment
stories.description: Donald Trump may have announced a last-minute ceasefire agreement with Iran, but it has done little to quieten a growing wave of Democratic lawmakers calling for his removal under the 25th Amendment.

stories.title: Iran continues to target Gulf countries despite ceasefire announcement
stories.description: Reporting from Doha for FRANCE 24, Hoda Abdel-Hamid explains that Iran's demands regarding the control over the Strait of Hormuz and the departure of US combat troops from the region are unlikely to be acceptable to Gulf countries.

stories.title: Trump says US forces to stay near Iran, ready for ‘next conquest’
stories.description: US threats against Iran and ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon cast doubt on fragile ceasefire efforts.

stories.title: Iran war day 41: What’s happening in Lebanon, Middle East and beyond?
stories.description: Lebanon has declared a national day of mourning after Israeli attacks killed at least 254 people in a single day.

stories.title: For Gulf states, Hormuz uncertainty casts shadow over US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Experts say Gulf countries are wary of the US agreeing to a deal that gives Iran leverage over the vital energy route.

stories.title: Trump slams NATO over Iran after meeting Rutte, renews Greenland threat
stories.description: US president meets NATO chief, expresses disappointment over member states failing to back war on Iran.

stories.title: US VP JD Vance says Lebanon is not part of the US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: US Vice President JD Vance says Lebanon is not part of the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump threatens action if Tehran fails to comply with deal
stories.description: UN human rights chief Volker Turk describes Israel's attacks on Lebanon as 'horrific', coming so soon after ceasefire.

stories.title: ‘Israel must be restrained for US-Iran ceasefire to hold’
stories.description: Former US counterterrorism chief Joe Kent had warned Israel could ‘sabotage’ the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Americans question Trump’s instinct on Iran war
stories.description: Americans are split on whether to trust Donald Trump's instinct-driven approach to the Iran conflict.

stories.title: JD Vance says Iran would be ‘dumb’ to let talks collapse over Lebanon
stories.description: The US vice president says Iran had a 'legitimate misunderstanding' about Lebanon's inclusion in this week's ceasefire.

stories.title: Trump administration signals it is mulling NATO withdrawal after Iran war
stories.description: The US president has lashed out at European partners for declining to contribute military forces to the war on Iran.

stories.title: Macron welcomes US-Iran ceasefire and urges Lebanon’s inclusion
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed the US-Iran ceasefire as “very good news".

stories.title: Trump threatens 50% tariffs on countries that supply Iran with weapons
stories.description: It's not clear under what legal authority Trump can tack on this tariff, and analysts called it an 'empty threat'.

stories.title: Devastation in Beirut after Israeli strikes reduce homes to rubble
stories.description: Hours after Israel hammered Beirut with multiple air strikes following the US-Iran ceasefire, residents searched for sur

stories.title: What to know about Israel’s strikes on Lebanon amid US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Israel unleashed a massive bombardment on Israel a day after the US and Iran reached a ceasefire agreement.

stories.title: Israeli army reports assassination of Hezbollah chief’s secretary in Beirut
stories.description: Ali Yusuf Harshi was described as a "close associate and personal advisor" to Sheikh Naim Qassem, "who played a central role in managing and securing his office"

stories.title: JAL to consider resumption of flights to Russia when conditions will be in place
stories.description: Japan Airlines said it suspended operations because the aviation insurance industry had stopped underwriting insurance for flights to Russia due to economic sanctions

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon could derail US-Iran ceasefire — Politico
stories.description: The publication cited an EU official who noted that Brussels, together with international partners, must speak out more actively on Lebanon and work to resume negotiations

stories.title: US demands concrete plans from NATO allies regarding Strait of Hormuz — news agency
stories.description: According to the news agency, several European allies of Washington have expressed their willingness to ensure the safety of the Strait of Hormuz since the ceasefire announcement

stories.title: Ceasefire offers chance to test US sincerity, Iranian envoy says
stories.description: "Given the past bitter experience of Washington repeatedly violating agreements, Iran is entering talks relying on careful verification efforts performed in the field conditions rather than on trust," Ali Mojtaba Rouzbahani said

stories.title: Israel not happy with US approach to brokering ceasefire with Iran — newspaper
stories.description: According to The Wall Street Journal, US President Donald Trump called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu shortly before announcing the ceasefire and presented him with a fait accompli

stories.title: ENEOS mum on potential restart of Russian oil imports
stories.description: The company pointed to economic sanctions introduced by Japan against Russia in view of the situation in Ukraine

stories.title: No winner or loser in war with Iran, with fate of ceasefire remaining unknown — newspaper
stories.description: Hurriyet noted that the ceasefire agreement was secured in part thanks to the efforts of the Turkish leadership

stories.title: Prior levels of oil supplies from Middle East require months to achieve — investor
stories.description: The break in the form of the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran "may end without even getting started," Kyle Shostak added

stories.title: US-Iran talks to kick off on Saturday and last several days — source
stories.description: The Serena Hotel is the most likely venue, the source added

stories.title: Iranian delegation to arrive in Pakistan on Thursday for talks with US — ambassador
stories.description: Reza Amiri Moghadam said it is planned despite the skepticism of the Iranian public due to the repeated ceasefire violations by Israel
